summary: Crisp sound‑board recording of a legendary second set – killer Sugar Magnolia, soaring Slipknot > Franklin’s Tower and a blistering Dancin’ make this 1976 show shine.
review: The SBD capture is exceptionally clean with crisp vocals, clear drums and a mix that lets Jerry’s lead guitar cut through without drowning the rest of the band.  Fans rave about the second‑set fireworks – Sugar Magnolia, Slipknot into Franklin’s Tower and an inspired Dancin’ – making this one of the most memorable ’76 tapes.

ai_rating.rationale: All but one review are 4‑5 stars, praising both the musical performance (standout second set) and the high‑quality soundboard source; only a minor keyboard‑mix comment tempers an otherwise glowing consensus.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Very clean mix with crisp vocals, audible drums and little treble harshness; Jerry sits forward in the mix but is praised for his tone.  No noticeable drop‑outs or crowd noise.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set is the highlight – high energy, tight transitions and inspired jams; reviewers note the overall setlist as average but the performance of these songs lifts the whole show.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Front‑of‑mix lead guitar that dominates the performance; praised for soaring tone and being “way out in front” of the band.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith is a bit frenetic and busy during Samson, but overall keyboards sit well in the mix.
band_member_comments.Drums: Crisp, clearly audible drumming praised as some of the best‑heard 1976 drum work.
